description: drop candidate #0 for Async import of the appStore packages

vars.prTitle: Async import of the appStore packages
vars.prBody:
## What does this PR do?

Async imports of all apps
vars.candidateFinding: {"comment":"WHAT: Eager execution of all dynamic imports defeats lazy loading and causes an initialization blowup. WHY: Assigning `import()` directly to object properties executes 30+ module loads concurrently at startup, spiking memory/CPU and risking immediate process crashes (Unhandled Promise Rejections) if any module fails to load. HOW: Wrap the dynamic imports in factory functions (e.g., `applecalendar: () => import(\"./applecalendar\")`) and invoke them at the call site (`const app = await appStore[app","location":"packages/app-store/index.ts:1","severity":"high","label":"performance","deliveryStatus":"sent","relevantFile":"packages/app-store/index.ts","relevantLinesStart":1,"relevantLinesEnd":1,"oneSentenceSummary":"Eager execution of all dynamic imports defeats lazy loading and causes an initialization blowup. WHY: Assigning `import()` directly to object properties executes 30+ module loads concurrently at startup, spiking memory/CPU and risking immediate process crashes (Unhandled Promise Rejections) if any module fails to load. HOW: Wrap the dynamic imports in factory functions (e.g., `applecalendar: () => import(\"./applecalendar\")`) and invoke them at the call site (`const app = await appStore[app"}

## What does this PR do?

vars.candidateFinding: {"comment":"Array.prototype.forEach is used with an async callback, which causes the inner calendar deletion operations to run detached without being awaited.\nThis creates a fire-and-forget execution where unhandled promise rejections are swallowed, and the outer function may return before the calendar deletions complete, leading to silent failures and ghost events left on users' calendars.\nReplace the `.forEach` chain with a `for...of` loop over the array, or use `await Promise.all(array.map(async ...))` t","location":"packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts:460","severity":"high","label":"bug","deliveryStatus":"sent","relevantFile":"packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts","relevantLinesStart":460,"relevantLinesEnd":460,"oneSentenceSummary":"Array.prototype.forEach is used with an async callback, which causes the inner calendar deletion operations to run detached without being awaited."}
vars.investigationSummary: Planner seed extracted files: packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx, packages/core/videoClient.ts, packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts, packages/app-store/index.ts, packages/core/CalendarManager.ts, packages/app-store/_utils/getCalendar.ts
vars.matchedGoldenComments: [{"gi":1,"comment":"The code uses forEach with async callbacks, which causes asynchronous operations (e.g., calendar/video event deletions, payment refunds) to run concurrently without being awaited. This 'fire-and-forget' behavior leads to unhandled promise rejections, race conditions, and incomplete cleanup, as surrounding try-catch blocks cannot properly handle errors from these unawaited promises. Replace forEach with for...of loops or Promise.all() with map() to ensure proper sequential execution and error handling.","severity":"Critical","reasoning":"Both comments identify the exact same underlying issue: using forEach with async callbacks creates fire-and-forget behavior where promises aren't awaited, leading to unhandled rejections and incomplete operations. Both mention the same solutions (for...of loops or Promise.all with map). The candidate focuses specifically on calendar deletions while the golden comment mentions broader async operations, but they're describing the same fundamental problem."}]
